English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Charles Downing Lay papers
Lay, Charles Downing, 1877-1956.
4477
11.6 cubic feet. 11.6 cubic feet.
13 mapcase folders. 13 mapcase folders.
1898-1956. Notebooks, papers, correspondence, photographs, drawings. Notebooks of architectural class notes and drawings; student papers; notebooks and sketchbooks on gardens; photographs of gardens and parks; architectural drawings and renderings; pen and ink drawings; and articles on architectural topics. Collection material in English 2094183
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
M. E. Warren
Date completed:
Apr. 1992
EAD encoding:
Julia Guarneri Aug. 2002
Date modified:
Jude Corina, February 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Landscape architect, architect, town planner.
Charles Downing Lay attended the School of Architecture at Columbia University from 1896 to 1900 and received a S.B. in Landscape Architecture from Harvard University's School of Landscape Architecture in 1902. From 1902 to 1948 he maintained a landscape architecture practice in New York City. He served as editor and publisher of Landscape Architecture with H. V. Hubbard and Robert Wheelwright. His projects included town planning for Albany, N.Y. and for the United States Housing Corporation in Erie and Butler, Pa.; parks in New York City, Troy, Albany, Schenectady, and Brooklyn, N.Y., Washington, D.C., and Stratford, Connecticut; school grounds in New York, Massachusetts, and Connecticut; subdivisions in New York, Massachusetts, Rhode Island, Connecticut, and New Jersey; and housing in New York City. He also was consulting architect to the 1939 New York World's Fair and worked on conservation efforts in the Housatonic River valley, founding the Housatonic Valley Planning Association in 1948.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Notebooks of architectural class notes and drawings, 1898, 1899; student papers; notebooks and sketchbooks on gardens; photographs of gardens and parks, especially Marine Park in Brooklyn; architectural drawings and renderings; pen and ink drawings; and articles on architectural topics. Also, papers relating to lectures; and club memberships, including the Century Club; miscellaneous correspondence; and condolence letters upon his death.
PUBLIC AREAS INCLUDED IN THE COLLECTION
Battery Park
Beaver Park, Albany, NY
Bridgford Park, Albany, NY
Butler, Pennsylvania
Cemetary of the Gate of Heaven, Mount Pleasant, NY
Central Park (A. Mitchell Memorial) New York City
Coney Island (New York City Fair) 1943
(Old) Essex County Golf Course, East Orange, New Jersey
Erie, Pennsylvania
Frear Park, Troy, New York
Loudon Heights, Albany, New York
Madison Square, New York City
Marine Park
North End Junior High School, Stratford, Connecticut
Overview Park, Pittsfield, Massachusetts
Philips Gallery, Washington, D.C.
Prospect Park, New York City
Recreation Terraces and Public Baths, New York City
Ridgefield Public School, Ridgefield, Connecticut
Rock Spring Country Club
St. Nicholas Houses, Manhattan, New York
Salem, Massachusetts
Seaside Park, Westbrook, Connecticut
Schenectady, New York
Sheepshead Bay Houses, Brooklyn, New York City
Stratford, Connecticut War Memorial and other
Tompkins Square, New York
Verona Civic Center, Verona, New Jersey
Washington Square
(Property of) Wheaton, Illinois
Woodruff Park, Stratford, Connecticut
PRIVATE ESTATES INCLUDED IN THE COLLECTION

George B. Agnew, Lewisboro, New York
Robert C. Anderson, Upper Montclair, New Jersey
J. Charles Andrews, Great Neck, Long Island
J. Percy Bartram, Caritas Island, Stamford, Connecticut

Annie Vietor [Victor?] Rumson
Maurice Wertheim, Cos Cob, Connecticut

Mrs. Willis D. Wood, Huntington, Long Island
Charles Yarnall, Newton Square, Pennsylvania
William Zinsser, Great Neck, Long Island
RELATED MATERIALS
Related collections: Oliver Ingraham Lay, Charles Downing Lay, and Lay family papers at the Archives of American Art, https://www.aaa.si.edu/collections/oliver-ingraham-lay-charles-downing-lay-and-lay-family-papers-6011.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Personal Box 1, Folders 1-4; Box 18, Folder 1; Box 19
Series II. Student Papers Box 1, Folders 5-14
Series III. Correspondence Box 1, Folders 15-39; Box 18, Folder 5-6
Series IV. Art and Artists Box 1, Folders 40-56
Series V. Private Homes and Estates Box 1, Folders 57-68
Series VI. Architecture, Planning, etc.: Housing, Parks, Committees and Other Box 2, Folders 1-49
Series VII. Writings Box 2, Folder 50 - Box 3, Folder 22; Box 18, Folder 10
Series VIII. Printed Material Boxes 4-7
Series IX. Photographs and negatives of private homes, estates, public areas and other Boxes 8-17
Series X. Oversize blueprints, sketches, drawings, paintings and photographs Box 19; Mapcase Folder 1-13
Series XI. Other Box 18, Folder 2, 3, 4, 7, 8, 9
Cite As:
Charles Downing Lay papers, #4477. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Photographs. Architectural drawings. Marine Park (New York, N.Y.) Architects and housing developers. Landscape architects. Gardens -- Design. Housing -- New York (N.Y.) Housing development -- New Jersey. Housing development -- Connecticut. Housing development -- Rhode Island. Housing development -- New York (State) School grounds -- Connecticut -- Stratford. School grounds -- Washington (D.C.) School grounds -- New York (State) City planning -- Pennsylvania -- Butler. City planning -- Pennsylvania -- Erie. City planning -- New York (N.Y.) Landscape architecture. Landscape architecture. United States Housing Corporation Housatonic Valley Planning Association New York World\'s Fair (Location of meeting: New York, N.Y.). Date of meeting or treaty signing: (1939-1940 :.)
